articles.summary: ChatGPT, Gemini, and Perplexity are now the kick off point for the buying journey, and the leading brands aren't guessing: they're running a system for content structure, refresh cadence, and off-site signals.

articles.title: GARTNER IS TELLING CMOS TO DOUBLE THEIR PR BUDGETS BY 2027
articles.summary: AI search engines cite non-paid sources far more than ads. About 94% of AI citations come from non-paid sources and 82% from earned media, with journalism accounting for 20-30%. Gartner predicts PR and earned media budgets will double by 2027 as LLMs replace traditional search discovery. Fresh content matters as over half of citations come from material published in the last 12 months, peaking within the first week. Brands with strong editorial presence and third-party credibility gain the most visibility.

articles.title: A $1,000 DOG GROOMING SESSION? THE PET WELLNESS INDUSTRY IS BOOMING
articles.summary: Pet grooming is now treated as a wellness practice. Owners are spending $500 - $11,000 per pet yearly on specialized trims, treatments, and custom meals. Rising costs are also leading some to travel for cheaper services. Pets are seen as family, and care focuses on prevention and routine. The industry is booming alongside human wellness trends and growing recognition of pets as sources of emotional support.

articles.title: TIME-SENSITIVE OFFERS IN GOOGLE ADS
articles.summary: Time-sensitive promotions in Google Ads need specific tactics because the platform decides which ad assets appear. Countdown customizers show the remaining time until an offer ends and create urgency. The countdown can display days and switch to hours and minutes on the final day. Promotion assets add a 25 character line that highlights a discount and can include start and end dates. Callout and sitelink assets can also highlight offers, though too many assets can reduce how often the promotion message appears.

articles.title: AUTOMATING LIFECYCLE EMAIL QA WITH CLAUDE
articles.summary: This post shares a Claude skill that automates first-pass QA for lifecycle emails, checking campaigns every 20 minutes or on demand. It reviews proofs in Gmail against 18 criteria, including subject lines, links, legal, and spam signals. Results post to Slack and update Asana subtasks with PASS, WARN, or BLOCK, letting reviewers focus on sign-off instead of auditing. The plugin link is provided in the comments and requires Asana, Slack, Claude Cowork, and Claude in Chrome.

articles.title: ALREADY RUNNING FB ADS? ADD TLDR TO YOUR MEDIA MIX.
articles.summary: TLDR reaches the technical decision makers who actually convert. MLOps Community found Meta drove 2x more registrations, but fewer

articles.title: actual attendees than TLDR. See how TLDR compares to Meta.
ANALYZE YOUR LAST 15 POSTS IN 10 MINUTES
articles.summary: Trust Architecture is a 4-stage framework that audits content based on how it builds trust rather than just engagement. It evaluates posts across credibility and commitment to see whether content actually moves audiences closer to buying. Most brands overproduce recognition content and skip the middle stages where trust is built. The system includes 4 AI prompts that analyze recent posts, diagnose trust gaps, and identify content changes that can improve pipeline.

articles.title: REDDIT'S IMPACT ON B2B SEARCH
articles.summary: Reddit now outranks many B2B SaaS vendors in Google results across major keyword categories. It beats competing vendors on 50-66% of shared keywords in three of four SaaS verticals and captures about 950K+ monthly searches before any vendor appears. Most wins come from generic category searches, which drive 77% of its search volume. Reddit also dominates longer queries, winning 73-100% of searches with six or more words. Just five subreddits generate 3,709 keyword rankings and over 1.1M monthly searches.

articles.title: ALWAYS WINNING: WHY COMPETITION IS ABOUT ENDURING BRAND RELEVANCE
articles.summary: Brands often react to market shifts by updating their identity or messaging, which can weaken recognition. Burberry illustrates this risk. Years of reinvention diluted its core identity. Uniqlo shows the opposite approach by evolving around a clear idea of LifeWear while maintaining a consistent role in the market. Leaders often default to visible change during uncertainty because it signals action. The strongest brands stay competitive by holding firm to a clear core and allowing thoughtful evolution around it.

articles.title: LINKEDIN ADS HAVE A REPUTATION PROBLEM
articles.summary: Most lead generation campaigns fail because they treat LinkedIn like search. Buyers on LinkedIn are not actively evaluating vendors. They are scrolling through industry content and orienting themselves around problems, which makes demo-first offers a poor match for their mindset. Assets like industry benchmarks and teardown style analyses consistently outperform direct demo requests with cold audiences. Demo offers only perform well after prospects have already interacted with the brand.

articles.title: STARTING A NEW PROJECT IS HARDER THAN YOU THINK
B2B LEAD MAGNETS CHANGED FOREVER WITH AI
articles.summary: B2B lead magnets are upgrading from static PDFs into “vibe-coded” tools.

articles.title: LINKS IN LINKEDIN POSTS ARE NOT ALGORITHMICALLY PUNISHED
articles.summary: LinkedIn confirmed that links, posting frequency, and scheduling tools don't reduce reach.
